name: Streator Onized
description_short: Streator Onized Credit Union is a state-chartered Illinois credit union established in 1940, serving over 36,000 members with $369.7M in assets across 1 branch and 4 ATMs.
description_long:
Streator Onized Credit Union was established in 1940 and is headquartered in Streator, Illinois. As a state-chartered credit union, it operates as a member-owned financial cooperative serving the Streator community and surrounding Illinois residents. The institution has grown to steward over $369.7 million in assets and maintains a membership base exceeding 36,000 members, supported by over 100 dedicated employees.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of financial products and services including deposit accounts (checking, savings, money market, and certificates of deposit), consumer lending (auto loans, personal loans, mortgages with multiple options), and credit cards. Members also have access to modern digital banking conveniences such as online banking, mobile apps for iOS and Android, mobile payments, bill pay, remote deposit capture, and e-statements. Additional services include IRA/KEOGH accounts, financial calculators, and educational resources through financial workshops and student scholarships.

Streator Onized operates with modest physical infrastructure—a single main office branch at 912 N Shabbona Street with a drive-thru window and 4 ATMs across its network. The credit union maintains standard lobby and drive-thru hours (currently open until 5:00 pm) and provides online service status monitoring. Their membership eligibility appears open to Illinois residents, with substantial account bases across share drafts (17,200+), regular savings (42,100+), share certificates/CDs (4,800+), and money market accounts (300+).

As a traditional credit union, Streator Onized serves members seeking community-based banking with personal service. However, prospective members should note the limited branch and ATM network compared to national banks, and current online reviews are not yet available on the platform to assess member satisfaction. The institution is appropriately categorized as a banking entity offering standard consumer credit union services rather than specialized lending or credit services. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Savings accounts (regular savings and money market accounts)
Mortgage loans (fixed rate and adjustable rate options)
Mobile banking apps for iOS and Android
Mobile payments and remote deposit capture
pros: Long-established institution founded in 1940 with substantial asset base of $369.7 million
Large membership base of 36,000+ members indicating stability and community trust
Comprehensive product offerings including mortgages (fixed and adjustable rate), auto loans, personal loans, and credit cards
Modern digital banking services including mobile apps for iOS and Android, online banking, bill pay, and remote deposit capture
Member education programs including financial workshops and student scholarships
State-chartered credit union structure typically offers member-ownership benefits and competitive rates
Drive-thru teller service available at main branch for convenient access
cons: Limited physical presence with only 1 branch and 4 ATMs, restricting in-person service accessibility
No member reviews currently available on the platform, limiting third-party satisfaction verification
Geographically restricted to Streator, Illinois location with unclear service area expansion
No information provided on current interest rates, loan terms, or fee structures for competitive comparison
best_for: Streator, Illinois residents seeking community-based banking with personal service
Credit union members prioritizing member-ownership structure and cooperative financial principles
Consumers seeking comprehensive lending products including mortgages and auto loans from a stable institution
Users comfortable with digital banking who prefer online and mobile banking over extensive branch networks
diagnosis: Streator Onized Credit Union is best for Illinois residents in or near Streator seeking full-service community banking with membership-ownership benefits and comprehensive loan products. The primary caveat is the limited branch/ATM network and lack of publicly available member reviews, making it important for prospective members to directly contact the institution to verify current rates, terms, and service quality before joining.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
